svg.text: Observable conditions, not action instructions. Triple reinforcement minimum.

g.text: Layer 1: eager — CLAUDE.md / GEMINI.md
Always in context. 7 rules + keyword routing + workflow gates + zones.
Even if nothing else loads, gates prevent the worst violations.
spec.md MUST EXIST
Layer 2: command preambles
At the top of every slash command. Workflow-specific enforcement.
Agent reads these before the first token of the workflow.
"design is clear" → NOT a spec
Layer 3: capabilities — loaded per step
build-loop, tdd, quality-gates, systematic-debug, scope-guard.
Add depth when loaded. Eager layer covers when they're not.
Gates run. Every task. No exceptions.
Layer 4: gate scripts — bash, not LLM
sage-verify.sh, sage-spec-check.sh, sage-hallucination-check.sh
Deterministic. Run FIRST, agent review SECOND. Can't be rationalized.
Bash before brains
Layer 5: self-learning — prevents repeat mistakes
WHEN/CHECK/BECAUSE prevention rules. Rule 0 memory search.
Scratch counter: 3+ approaches = automatic gotcha trigger.
Agents remember their mistakes

svg.text: File existence is binary. The agent can't rationalize a file into existence.
Name the excuses. The agent reads its own rationalization and has to proceed anyway.
